mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals consumer demand momentum that directly drives corporate earnings revisions and equity valuations, producing same-direction S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between RSAFS monthly growth and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) or the sign flips in more than one distinct subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) drops below 4.5%, the labor market becomes sufficiently tight that wage inflation pressures force the Federal Reserve into a more hawkish stance, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: In any 2-year window after UNRATE drops below 4.5%, the proportion of negative 5-day forward returns is less than 55% or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 5.0% represents a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases the discount rate for future equity cash flows and raises corporate borrowing costs, reliably producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with high policy rates
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 40% of instances, or the average cumulative return across triggered periods is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) grows strongly but retail sales (RSAFS) stagnate or decline, the divergence signals unintentional inventory accumulation and weakening final demand, foreshadowing production cutbacks and negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In a linear model regressing 20-day S&P 500 return on INDPRO growth, RSAFS growth, and their interaction, the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) with the predicted negative sign over any rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead residential investment and broader economic activity by several months; sustained month-over-month growth in HOUST signals an expanding economy that supports corporate profits and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The 5-year rolling correlation between HOUST monthly growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative or not significantly positive (p > 0.10) in more than half of the historical periods.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) rises rapidly while GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates, it indicates a productivity slowdown that squeezes profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns as the market reprices earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 0.5% and PAYEMS monthly growth > 0.2%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion when growth slows but employment remains strong
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the proportion of negative 5-day forward returns is less than 55%, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), over any full cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year inflation in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 3.0% forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising the risk-free rate and depressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: After inflation exceeds 3.0% YoY, the 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 45% of triggered cases, or the average return across all triggered periods is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In the early expansion phase following a recession, simultaneous positive three-month growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirms a self-reinforcing demand recovery that drives strong corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both show positive 3-month growth
failure_condition: In early expansion periods (first 12 months after NBER trough), when both proxies show positive 3-month growth, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the proportion of positive returns is less than 60%.
